text.title: Agent Pitfalls and Best Practices
text.summary: Common agent-generated mistakes in token pipeline setup and the rules that prevent them.

antipattern.description: Agent writes Style Dictionary transforms against the deprecated v3 API.
antipattern.reason: Style Dictionary v4 has a different transform API; v3 transforms silently produce no output or malformed files.

antipattern.description: Generated SCSS maps use Dart Sass-deprecated syntax (e.g., misplaced !default).
antipattern.reason: Newer Dart Sass deprecations surface only at build time; lint generated SCSS before committing.

antipattern.description: Color-space metadata (sRGB vs. P3) is dropped during token transform.
antipattern.reason: Transforms silently coerce color spaces; require explicit color-space metadata in the source and validate output format.

antipattern.description: Agent invents Tokens Studio capabilities (e.g., "math expressions") not in current docs.
antipattern.reason: Hallucinated features produce pipeline configs that break at runtime; validate all claimed features against current Tokens Studio documentation.

antipattern.description: Agent prunes "unused" tokens automatically using static analysis.
antipattern.reason: Static analysis misses theming, runtime, and per-tenant token references; auto-pruning causes silent regressions.

rule.statement: Snapshot-test rendered components after every token change, not just raw value diffs.
rule.rationale: Raw value diffs miss visual regressions caused by aliasing, transforms, or platform-specific rendering differences.

rule.statement: Validate token files against the W3C draft format on every export to keep future tooling compatibility.
rule.rationale: Early validation prevents a forced re-migration when the ecosystem converges on the standard.

rule.statement: Never auto-apply token PRs that change a primary color without a staged rollout and visual diff review.
rule.rationale: A wrong primary color rebrands the product instantly across all platforms simultaneously.
